{{Infobox book
| name = Of Dawn and Darkness
| slug = ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness
| precedes = [[Of Darkness and Dawn (novel)|Of Darkness and Dawn]]
| follows = [[Of Shadow and Sea (novel)|Of Shadow and Sea]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2015
| isbn = 9781713530251
| publisher = Audible Studios on Brilliance
| pages = 362
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The story follows the interconnected lives of Jyrine Tessella Marten{{!}}Jyrine Tessella and Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] amidst a world being manipulated by ancient, cosmic entities known as the [[Great Elders]]. Twenty years ago, a young [[Jyrine]] witnessed a ritual in a coastal temple where an emerald jewel bound itself to her, marking her as a figure of immense importance. [[Eleven]] years later, [[Jyrine]] uses her influence to convince the [[Sleepless]] cabal that [[Calder]]—an exiled [[Blackwatch]] member—is a valuable asset. At this time, [[Calder]] is working off a debt under the supervision of [[Andel Petronus]], commanding the ship *[[The Testament]]* and transporting the prisoner [[Urzaia]] to an arena. During this journey, [[Calder]]'s crew faces sentient geometric flames in the [[Aion Sea]], and [[Calder]] learns of [[Urzaia]]’s history as a rebel [[Champion]] subjected to alchemical testing. 

In the present day, the political landscape is in chaos following [[the Emperor]]'s death. [[Calder]] finds himself thrust into a position of power, intended to serve as a figurehead Emperor using the [[Optasia]]—a powerful throne—to monitor the world. However, the [[Imperial]] Palace is being consumed by rapidly regenerating [[Elder flesh]]. As [[Calder]] navigates his new role as [[Imperial Steward]], he faces constant threats from the [[Consultant assassins]] and the [[Sleepless cult]]. His personal life is equally fractured; his wife, [[Jerri]], has become a pawn of the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], working to prevent certain individuals from meeting [[Calder]] and eventually murdering an assassin named [[Lucan]] in a violent display of her [[Vessel]]'s power. 

[[Calder]]’s journey takes him from the ruins of the ship *[[The Eternal]]* to the abandoned, monster-infested town of [[Silverreach]]. In [[Silverreach]], after being captured by the [[Sleepless]], [[Calder]] and his allies—including the newly recruited gunsmith [[Duster]] ([[Dalton Foster]])—manage a daring escape. The crew's path is further complicated by cosmic anomalies, including a massive [[crack in the sky]] and a sudden displacement into a mysterious, massive [[library]]. Within this [[library]], [[Calder]] encounters the [[Great Elder]] Ach'magut{{!}}Ach’magut, who prophesies that [[Calder]] is a 'cosmic deviation' destined for the imperial throne. 

As the conflict escalates, [[Calder]] leads military strikes against the [[Consultants]] and faces off against the powerful [[Regent]] [[Jorin Maze-walker]]. During a climactic battle, [[Calder]] is aided by [[Baldesar Kern]] and a group of [[Champions]]. Despite the manipulation of the [[Great Elders]], who seek to use [[Calder]] to further their own ends, [[Calder]] remains defiant. Following a hard-won victory for the [[Imperialist Guilds]] on [[Gray Island]], [[Calder]] sets his sights on seizing control of The Empire{{!}}the Empire, even as the true nature of the [[Great Elders]]' presence in their world remains a looming mystery.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Twenty years ago, young Jyrine Tessella Marten{{!}}Jyrine Tessella accompanies her father to a golden temple in a coastal town to witness a ritual performed by a group of robed figures. During the ceremony, [[Jyrine]] experiences a series of vivid, disturbing visions involving a giant worm and other grotesque imagery, eventually communicating with a mysterious voice in a void. The ritual concludes when an emerald jewel manifests and binds itself to [[Jyrine]], leading her father to tell her that she will be very important someday.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After being taken hostage by the Consultant assassins{{!}}Consultant assassin [[Meia]], Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder attempts to use his ship's [[Lyathatan]] to fight back but is quickly incapacitated by a poison needle. A tense standoff ensues between [[Meia]] and the [[Blackwatch Guild Head]], [[Bliss]], who reveals that [[Calder]] is needed to sit on a throne. The confrontation is interrupted when a mysterious distortion of space tears the approaching ship, [[The Eternal]], in half.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Eleven]] years ago, sixteen-year-old Jyrine Tessella Marten{{!}}Jyrine Tessella uses a fire transmission to convince the [[Sleepless]] cabal that [[Calder]], an exiled [[Blackwatch]] member, could be a valuable asset. She reveals that [[Calder]] has the approval of the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], prompting the cabal to seek guidance from their other patron, Ach'magut{{!}}Ach’magut. Despite [[Jyrine]]'s knowledge that Ach'magut{{!}}Ach’magut is dead, the cabal suggests his death is merely a minor inconvenience to their plans.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After the destruction of the ship *[[The Eternal]]*, [[Calder]] uses his connection to the [[Lyathatan]] to rescue survivors, including [[General Teach]] and [[Cheska Bennett]]. [[Bliss]] reveals that the [[Imperial]] Palace has been taken over and explains that [[Calder]] is intended to be a figurehead Emperor who uses the [[Optasia]], a powerful throne, to monitor the world. Meanwhile, in the transformed [[Imperial]] Palace, [[Jerri]] is held captive by a mysterious blind man within a room made of living flesh, where they await an unknown purpose involving a silver cage that acts as bait.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Eleven]] years ago, [[Calder Marten]] is released from the [[Imperial]] Palace by guards after his father's execution and assigned a [[Luminian]] officer, [[Andel Petronus]], to supervise him while he works off a five-thousand-goldmark debt. Accompanied by his friend [[Jerri]], [[Calder]] boards his ship, [[The Testament]], only to discover it is being used to transport a mysterious prisoner named [[Urzaia]] to an arena. The crew includes two legendary [[Imperial]] [[Champions]], [[Eight]] and [[Nine]], who are tasked with guarding the large cage containing the prisoner.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After successfully navigating the ruined ship *[[The Eternal]]* into the Capital using a [[Lyathatan]] and a [[Guild]] ship, [[Calder]] and his companions arrive to find the city in a state of panic. They discover that the [[Imperial]] Palace is being consumed by rapidly regenerating [[Elder flesh]] growing from the [[Emperor's quarters]]. [[General Teach]] uses her powerful [[sword]], [[Tyrfang]], to temporarily clear the growth, but the flesh retaliates, forcing the group to seek refuge within the palace while [[Bliss]] prepares to investigate the phenomenon.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|While navigating the [[Aion Sea]], [[Calder]] and [[Jerri]] discuss their journey and personal histories before being interrupted by a sudden phenomenon of geometric, sentient flames orbiting the ship. The [[Champions]] [[Nine]] and [[Eight]] engage the fiery entities using their unique powers, resulting in [[Nine]] being burned and thrown overboard by [[Calder]] to save him. After the threat subsides, the prisoner [[Urzaia]] explains that the fires were meant to guide lost ships and suggests a secret meeting with [[Calder]] later that night.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After being visited in a dream by the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], who reveals that [[Calder]] is being manipulated as part of a larger scheme, [[Calder]] attends a meeting with several [[Guild Heads]]. The leaders discuss their recent assassination of a [[Regent]] and plan to solidify their power by announcing [[Calder]] as the [[Imperial Steward]] while goading the [[Luminian]] [[Guild]] into a conflict. Meanwhile, [[Bliss]] discovers that the [[Elder flesh]] surrounding the [[Emperor's quarters]] is being sustained by the [[Optasia]] and prepares to lead a group to breach it.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|While being interrogated by [[Calder]], the prisoner [[Urzaia]] recounts his history as a [[Champion]] who was subjected to brutal alchemical testing and mental conditioning. He describes how he eventually rebelled against [[the Emperor]] after discovering The Empire{{!}}the Empire's cruelty toward northern villages, leading to his capture and death sentence in the [[Axciss]] arena. Moved by [[Urzaia]]'s perspective, [[Calder]] promises to return one day to rescue him.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jerri]] [[Marten]] is revealed to be working with the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]], who promises her power in exchange for obedience as she defends herself against an [[Imperial Guard]] assault. Meanwhile, [[Calder]] uses his Awakened blade to help [[General Teach]] and [[Bliss]] breach an [[Elder wall]], leading to a chaotic magical clash that triggers the [[Optasia]] and causes the sky to distort. After surviving an assassination attempt by a [[Champion]], [[Calder]] uses his influence to protect [[Meia]], a [[Consultant]] who saved his life, by claiming she is a member of his crew.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|While sailing to [[Silverreach]], [[Calder]] and his crew discover through a stolen journal that the town may be the site of a [[Great Elder]]'s revival. Upon arriving, they find the town eerily abandoned and encounter a young boy who warns them of multi-eyed, ten-legged spiders. The group is soon ambushed by these creatures and a cult of the [[Sleepless]], leaving [[Calder]] to call upon his ship's [[Lyathatan]] for help as they are captured.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|While recovering in an alchemical bath, [[Calder]] discusses the investigation into his attacker and his new role as [[Imperial Steward]] with [[General Teach]] and [[Meia]]. [[Calder]] later visits his wife, [[Jerri]], in a dungeon cell, where she claims the [[Great Elders]] have orchestrated his rise to power and warns that the sky is about to crack. Following a massive tremor, the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]] appears to [[Jerri]] in her cell, revealing that the survival of the assassin [[Shera]] has disrupted the [[Elders]]' plans and tasking [[Jerri]] with preventing [[Shera]] from meeting [[Calder]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After being captured by the [[Sleepless cult]] in [[Silverreach]], [[Jerri]] uses her magical abilities to intimidate her captors while [[Calder]] and [[Andel]] coordinate an escape from their prison cell. Assisted by a skilled [[Soulbound gunsmith]] named [[Duster]], the group fights through [[Inquisitors]] and cultists to liberate several female prisoners. The chapter concludes with the survivors escaping on the ship *[[The Testament]]*, where [[Calder]] recruits [[Duster]] as a gunner and learns his true identity is [[Dalton Foster]].}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After delivering an improvised speech to reassure the panicked nobility about a [[crack in the sky]], [[Calder]] learns that [[Magister]] [[Guild Head]] [[Mekendi Maxeus]] has been murdered by suspected [[Consultants]]. While [[General Teach]] prepares for a retaliatory strike against the [[Consultant's Guild]], [[Calder]] suspects the assassination is a trap orchestrated by the [[Elders]]. Seeking clarity, [[Calder]] visits the Emperor's personal rooms{{!}}Emperor's chambers to inspect the [[Optasia]], only to discover a hidden presence within the room.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Urzaia]], a gladiator sentenced to death, survives his three-hundredth match by defeating the [[Houndmaster]] and his sand-[[construct]] hounds. Meanwhile, [[Calder]] and [[Jerri]] observe the fight from the stands while scouting the arena's exits to plan a future rescue. The chapter concludes with [[Urzaia]] declaring his intent to live until a promised freedom is fulfilled, unaware that [[Calder]] is watching him.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After an assassin named [[Lucan]] attacks the [[Imperial]] Palace, [[Calder]] manages to capture him and discovers that [[Lucan]] intended to warn him about the dangers of using the [[Optasia]], an ancient throne that could invite [[Great Elder]] possession. [[Calder]] brings together [[Lucan]], his associate [[Meia]], and his wife [[Jerri]] to debate the device's [[fate]], but the meeting turns violent when [[Jerri]] attempts to eliminate [[Shera]]'s allies. In a final act of desperation, [[Jerri]] uses her [[Vessel]] to kill [[Lucan]], leaving [[Calder]] in horror as she is taken back into custody.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Six years ago, Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]] attempts to illicitly purchase explosives from an alchemist named [[Lampson]] in a back alley, but the deal fails due to [[Lampson]]'s distrust of Navigators. Shortly after, [[Calder]] encounters a mysterious young woman named [[Petal]] living in a mobile alchemical workshop disguised as a metal bin. After learning she is an unregistered apprentice fleeing her past, [[Calder]] agrees to take her aboard his ship to help him manufacture the munitions he needs for an upcoming mission.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]], accompanied by [[Andel]] and [[General Teach]], visits the legendary [[Baldesar Kern]] to request the support of the [[Champions]] for an upcoming military strike against the [[Consultants]]. [[Kern]] refuses, revealing that the Champion’s Guild{{!}}Champion's Guild has effectively dissolved following [[the Emperor]]'s death and that he does not trust [[Calder]]'s leadership. Following this failure, [[Teach]] leads a weary [[Calder]] through the [[Imperial]] Palace to the [[Emperor's personal armory]] to prepare him for the impending battle.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] and his crew prepare to rescue the gladiator [[Urzaia]] by planting alchemical charge{{!}}alchemical charges around the [[Axciss]] arena, aided by [[Andel]]'s unexpected cooperation. During [[Urzaia]]'s match against a fire-breathing [[Cinderbeast]], the creature's flames accidentally ignite the hidden explosives, causing a massive structural collapse. Amidst the chaos and crumbling stone, [[Urzaia]] defeats the beast with his [[Awakened weapons]] and joins [[Calder]]'s crew, jokingly offering to serve as a cook rather than a guard.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|As the Imperialist fleet approaches [[Gray Island]], [[General Teach]] and [[Bliss]] depart on a winged mount to confront [[Jorin Curse-breaker]], the [[Regent of the South]], who has arrived at the [[Consultant headquarters]]. [[Calder]] attempts to use [[the Emperor]]'s crown to command the [[Consultants]], but his efforts fail as the [[Imperial]] landing forces are met with deadly traps and ambushes. After witnessing [[Teach]] struggling in a violent duel against [[Jorin]]'s overwhelming power, [[Calder]] decides to go ashore to intervene.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After months of tedious sailing, [[Calder]] and his crew are intercepted by [[Imperial ships]] led by Under-lieutenant [[Mora Belyard]], who reveals that The Empire{{!}}the Empire is facing a massive surge in Elder activity. To avoid capture and retrieve [[Urzaia]], the crew docks at the abandoned town of [[Silverreach]], where [[Urzaia]] has gone ashore alone to hide. While attempting to find him, [[Calder]] and his companions experience a sudden lapse in consciousness and wake up inside a massive, mysterious [[library]].}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|During a brutal battle between [[Jarelys Teach]] and the [[Regent]] [[Jorin Maze-walker]], [[Calder]] uses his [[Awakened sword]] and [[the Emperor]]'s protective armor to survive [[Jorin]]'s attacks. [[Calder]] enters a vision where the [[Great Elder]] [[Kelarac]] offers him allies in exchange for executing [[Shera]], but [[Calder]] defiantly refuses the deal. Just as [[Jorin]] prepares to kill him, [[Baldesar Kern]] and a group of [[Champions]] arrive to intervene, having been hired by a mysterious blindfolded Reader to give [[Calder]] a chance.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|After being transported to a massive [[library]], [[Calder]] and his crew encounter the [[Great Elder]] Ach'magut{{!}}Ach’magut, who reveals that [[Calder]] is a cosmic deviation and prophesies his rise to the imperial throne. Following this encounter, the crew flees [[Silverreach]] just as the town is destroyed by a massive explosion. [[Calder]] shares his past with the crew and declares his intention to seize control of The Empire{{!}}the Empire once [[the Emperor]] passes.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Following the [[Imperialist Guilds]]' victory on [[Gray Island]], [[Calder]] attempts to intercept a fleeing [[Consultant]] vessel, but the ship escapes using [[Bastion's Veil]]. During a moment of respite, [[Bliss]] explains that the [[Great Elders]] are trapped in their world and were lured there by an unknown force. The narrative then shifts back five years, depicting [[Calder]] and his crew arriving at the Capital to find it in chaos following the death of [[the Emperor]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jerri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elderspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Emperor (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Andel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Urzaia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Petal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Lyathatan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Foster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Meia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Andel Petronus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Bliss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[General Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Shuffles]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Urg’naut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Urzaia Woodsman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Shera]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jarelys Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Mekendi Maxeus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Reader (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Lucan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Duster]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jorin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tharlos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Nine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Loreli]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sadesthenes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jyrine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Eight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Mr. Valette]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Cheska]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Navigator]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Alsa Grayweather]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kthanikahr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Handmaiden]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Alagaeus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Baldesar Kern]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Killer]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Optasia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Blackwatch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Gray Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Aion Sea]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Imperial Palace (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Imperial Guards]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Silverreach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Capital (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Aion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Izyria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sleepless]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Imperial Guard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Consultant's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Champions]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Axciss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Navigator’s Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Regents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Consultants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Inquisitors]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Navigators (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Magisters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Architects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Witnesses (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Empire (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Luminian Order]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Luminian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Estyr Six]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Ones]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Emperor's quarters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Independents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Ach’magut’s Inquisitors]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Guild of Magisters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Greenwardens]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Watchmen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Alchemists]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Champion’s Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Guild Heads]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Emperor's personal rooms]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Reading]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Testament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Tyrfang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[The Eternal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Heart of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Spear of Tharlos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sandborn Hydra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac’s mark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Navigator’s ship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elder wall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Awakened blade (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[emerald (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Ach'magut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kameira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Soulbound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Elder (The Elder Empire: Sea)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Magister]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Reader’s burn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Champion]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL20875441W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
